gate: S1a IMPL owner-confirmed correct, VERIFY (F-001 manual check) still pending; S5 W1 + W6 + W3 + W4 done and live. Both halves of the registration join now exist in production and NOTHING JOINS THEM — that is the next task. W2/W5 outstanding.
layout: one repository, several containers: services/core -> growth-core:3376 (ClusterIP only); services/web -> growth-web:3377 planned at S5

database.name: growth_core
database.note: Roles split 2026-07-20. The runtime role holds DML grants only and owns nothing, so it cannot disable the decision_artefact append-only trigger. Migrations 001-003 applied in production.

exchanges.growth.events: topic, durable, routing key = event type. Produced by growth-core's ingest drain, now carrying growth.auth_redirect.initiated.v1 from bazos-service (W4). NO CONSUMER BOUND — a topic exchange discards unmatched messages, so clicks are currently being dropped. Bind a durable queue before relying on them.
exchanges.auth.events: topic, durable. Produced by auth-microservice (W3). Queue growth.auth-registrations is bound to auth.user.registered.v1 and accumulating; growth-core does not consume it yet.

broker.note: A topic exchange discards messages with no matching binding, so a consumer must declare its queue and binding BEFORE the producer it depends on goes live. growth.auth-registrations was declared ahead of W3's deploy for exactly this reason. It has no consumer and grows unbounded.

notes: 149 passing tests. Verified in production 2026-07-20: /health ok, POST /ingest/events returns 202 then 200 on replay, an event posted end to end arrived verbatim in a queue bound to growth.events and its buffer row reached status=published, and the runtime role was refused DISABLE TRIGGER and UPDATE. Drain runs every 5s; retention sweep at 03:00 Europe/Prague.
